items.title: Freeze unrelated variables explicitly
items.avoid: Differentiating an expression while forgetting which symbols are independent coordinates.
items.prefer: State which variable you differentiate and treat every other symbol as a constant for that partial.
items.why: Correct partials are the atoms of gradients; one wrong frozen variable corrupts the entire update direction.
items.example: For L(w, b) = (w·x + b − y)², write ∂L/∂w = 2(w·x + b − y)·x with b and x fixed as constants w.r.t. w.

items.title: Verify partials on low-dimensional toy problems
items.avoid: Trusting autograd output on a full model without ever checking a hand-computed 2-parameter example.
items.prefer: Compute ∂L/∂w and ∂L/∂b symbolically or numerically on a tiny dataset and match framework `.grad` values.
items.why: Toy checks catch sign errors, forgotten chain factors and wrong loss reductions before they waste GPU hours.
items.example: For one sample (x=1, y=3), w=1, b=0, MSE loss (w+b−3)² gives ∂L/∂w = 2(−2)·1 = −4; confirm `w.grad` matches.
